sutta: the buddha said monks these 4 persons are found existing in the world what for he who is ben neither on his own prophet nor on the prophet of another he who is bent on another's prophet but not on his own he who is bent on his own prophet not anothers and he who is bent on the prophet both of himself and of another just as monks a firebrand from a funeral pyre blazing at both ends and in the middle smeared with dung serves no purpose as field in village or as timber in forest using such a figure do i speak of this person who is banned neither on his own prophet nor on another's then monks this person who is bent on another's prophet but not his own is more excellent and exalted of these 2 persons again monks this person who is bent on his own prophet not another's is more excellent and exalted of these 3 persons whereas he who is bent on the prophet both of self and of another is of these 4 persons chief and best topmost highest and supreme just as monks from a cow comes milk from milk cream from cream butter from butter ghee from ghee the skimmings of ghee and that is reckoned the best even so this person who is bent on his own prophet as well as on the prophet of another is of these 4 persons chief and best top most highest and supreme these 4 persons monks are found existing in the world addis this is about uh refers to the spiritual path because the next suta sutra number 96 explains that a person bent on is on his own prophet not on another's prophet is a person who is who practices the restraint of passion hatred and delusion but does not encourage another person to practice the restraint of passion hatred and delusion so you can see that
commentary: this suta is concerned with the spiritual path and you notice and the first 1 who you he's been neither to his own prophet or on another's prophet that means he does not cultivate himself and does not encourage another person to cultivate himself and this is a useless person just like a piece of wood from a funeral party it's already smoking and burning it can't be used as a fuel and it also cannot be used as timber then the second person is slightly better it's like the second person is bent on another's prophet but not on his own that means he encourages another person to cultivate himself but he does not cultivate his own self he's slightly better and the third person who is even better is a person who is bent on his own prophet but not another's that means he cultivates himself but he does not encourage another person to improve himself and the best is the person who cultivates himself as well as encourages other people to improve themselves spiritually so from this suta you find an interesting point here is that the second person is uh the the 1 who is uh spent on another's prophet but none of his own is is uh lower than the person who is banned on his own prophet but not another's in other words the buddha is saying that if we practice the spiritual path ourselves [Music] but not encourage others to practice this is better than another person who does not practice to cultivate himself but he encourages other people to practice why because this is something that some people uh find difficult to understand because some people they have the idea that a person who cultivates himself for example a person who meditates stays alone to meditate to meditate and that he's selfish whereas another person who is helping other people but not himself he is better but in the buddha this is not soul because basically most people uh we love ourselves the most of all the persons in the world because of our ego there's nobody here we uh are more concerned with than the self this is pointed out in another suta later we'll come across so if we say that we are only concerned with other people's goodness but not of ourselves and trying to think to go around helping other people but not yourself and then in a way you are uh kind of um bluffing yourself like because uh there's no person more important to you 1 than your own self and we have to recognize this so if we uh cultivate ourselves and then later we can help other people also but of course if you can do both that is the very best now we come to another suta
